summary: Added a Stage Queue to Route Replay so operators can see the current and upcoming filtered replay stages, then jump directly into the Event Proof lens from a compact playable itinerary.

queries: timeline queue UI upcoming steps design pattern
Material Design lists component queues filter chips
game UI quest log stage queue upcoming milestones design
pattern_notes: Filter chips are compact controls for selecting or narrowing visible content.
Stepper and timeline patterns work best when users can navigate freely across stages.
Quest logs and timelines should expose state, history, and recovery context without forcing operators to read an entire trail.

ui_contract: Render a four-card Stage Queue inside Route Replay using the active filtered replay list.
Mark the active stage as Now, wrap upcoming stages when the filtered list is shorter than the route tail, and keep glyphs aligned with event type.
Clicking a queued stage updates Route Replay index and Event Proof focus without mutating snapshot data.
Replay Lens filters, NEXT, latest, and play/pause continue to operate inside the same filtered stage list.
The queue collapses to one column on narrow viewports and preserves the static no-build repo contract.

red_route.evidence: Before implementation, Path Map had 0 .path-replay-queue panels and 0 data-path-replay-jump controls while Route Replay was present at Stage 1 of 64.

green_route.evidence: Rendered one Stage Queue with 4 jump cards, first card marked Now, stage jumps 0-3, mixed service and outcome glyphs, stats 64 events / 38 proofs / 0% run, and no bad rectangles.

filtered_jump.evidence: Service filter narrowed the replay to 6 shown stages; clicking queue jump 2 moved the title to Stage 3 of 6 with four service queue cards and 6/64 lens stats.

filtered_playback.evidence: Outcome filter moved to Stage 4 of 31, play changed the badge to playing and the button to PAUSE, then auto-advanced the queue Now card to Stage 5 of 31 before pause restored PLAY.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports rendered one queue with 4 items, no Route Replay overflow, document width equal to client width, and single-column queue layout below the mobile breakpoint.
